web.quote: Black rot and frogeye leaf spot are phases of a widespread and damaging disease of apple ... The fruit rot phase is called black rot and on the leaf it is called frogeye leaf spot.
web.source_title: Black Rot and Frogeye Leaf Spot of Apple (Ohioline, OSU)
web.claim: NOT web-verified as a look-alike pair: sources document black rot <-> frogeye leaf spot (the same fungus on fruit vs leaf), not apple scab vs black rot confusion.

web.quote: However, it is hard to identify a disease on the basis of a photograph of a single leaf.
web.source_title: Apple Scab or Red Cedar Rust - Ask Extension

web.quote: Diseased limbs show symptoms that resemble fire blight.
web.source_title: Black Rot Disease in Apples | Extension | West Virginia University

web.quote: Weir's spruce cushion rust (Chrysomyxa weirii) is unique in that it is autoecious and does not require separate hosts to complete its life cycle ... laboratory analysis of infected needles is critical for accurate diagnosis and subsequent control strategies.
web.source_title: Spruce Needle Rust — University of Illinois Extension, Home, Yard & Garden Pest Newsletter

web.quote: Symptoms of Volutella blight can be confused with those of boxwood blight, Cylindrocladium buxicola
web.source_title: Volutella Blight, or Pseudonectria Canker / Home and Landscape / UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: Black rot can also predispose vegetables to other rot diseases such as bacterial soft rot.
web.source_title: Black Rot of Crucifers – Wisconsin Horticulture (University of Wisconsin-Madison Division of Extension)

common-bean__beet-curly-top-virus__commo.member_b: Common Bacterial  Blight Of Beans, Fuscous Blight

common-bean__common-bacterial--blight-of.member_a: Common Bacterial  Blight Of Beans, Fuscous Blight

web.quote: There are three bacterial diseases of bean which are very similar, Common Blight (Xanthomonas campestris pv. phaseoli), Halo Blight (Pseudomonas syringae pv. phaseolicola), and Fuscous Blight (a variant of X. campestris pv. phaseoli). ... The symptoms for all three diseases are very similar, and it generally takes an experienced diagnostician to differentiate them.
web.source_title: Bacterial Bean Blights - UConn IPM (University of Connecticut Integrated Pest Management)

common-bean__common-bacterial--blight-of.member_a: Common Bacterial  Blight Of Beans, Fuscous Blight
Common Bacterial  Blight Of Beans, Fuscous Blight

web.quote: Can be confused with soil splash, brown spot and halo blight
web.source_title: Dry Edible Bean Disease Diagnostic Series | NDSU Agriculture (Extension)

web.quote: Immature lesions are not diagnostic and can easily be confused with Gray Leaf Spot or Eye spot.
web.source_title: Anthracnose in Corn | CropWatch | University of Nebraska-Lincoln

corn__anthracnose__white-ear-rot-and-see.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: In early stages, several foliar diseases on corn may look similar to GLS:
web.source_title: Gray Leaf Spot in Corn | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Based on the fact that they both produce rusty looking pustules on the leaves, producers may actually be confusing common rust with southern rust, especially when they are not occurring side-by-side on the same leaf.
web.source_title: Southern Rust and Common Rust on Hybrid Corn: How to Tell Them Apart | Agronomic Crops Network (Ohio State University Extension)

corn__common-corn-rust__white-ear-rot-an.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: Corn smut can be confused with other corn molds and fungal ear rot organisms that can produce dangerous mycotoxins.
web.source_title: Scout for Corn Molds and Ear Rots This Harvest Season

corn__corn-smut__white-ear-rot-and-seedl.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.source_title: An Overview of Goss's Bacterial Wilt and Blight - Crop Protection Network

corn__downy-mildew__white-ear-rot-and-se.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: Mold may be very pale in some cases, causing it to be confused with other ear rots.
web.source_title: Gibberella Ear Rot | Pioneer Seeds Agronomy

corn__fusarium-graminearum-schwabe__whit.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: In recent years, an increase in NCLB incidence in some areas and reduction in the number of corn hybrids that produce the recognizable 'freckles' symptom of Goss's wilt has made differentiating these two diseases more challenging.
web.source_title: An Overview of Goss's Bacterial Wilt and Blight

corn__goss--bacterial-wilt__white-ear-ro.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: In its early stages, gray leaf spot may be confused with other foliar fungal diseases, including northern corn leaf blight, anthracnose leaf blight, eyespot and common rust.
web.source_title: Field Facts: Gray Leaf Spot | Inputs & Insights | Corteva Agriscience

corn__gray-leaf-spot__white-ear-rot-and-.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

corn__northern-corn-leaf-blight__white-e.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

corn__southern-corn-rust__white-ear-rot-.member_b: White Ear Rot And Seedling Blight Of Maize

web.quote: Anthracnose is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Angular leaf spot, caused by Pseudomonas psyringae pv. lachrymans
web.source_title: Anthracnose of Cucurbits | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Several different fungal and bacterial pathogens cause these symptoms and these diseases may occur simultaneously. It is challenging to tell them apart without a laboratory diagnosis.
web.source_title: Key to Common Problems of Cucumbers | University of Maryland Extension

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: Alternaria leaf blight, caused by Alternaria cucumerina... Angular leaf spot, caused by Pseudomonas psyringae pv. lachrymans...
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: The spots can be mistaken for insect damage or anthracnose.
web.source_title: Corynespora cassiicola (target spot of cucurbits) - Bugwoodwiki

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Anthracnose, caused by Colletotrichum orbiculare
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Gummy stem blight and black rot can look like other plant diseases that affect cucurbits; however, this pathogen can be distinguished by stem and vine lesions near nodes, black fruiting bodies on dead tissue along with the characteristic brown gummy exudate from wounds near lesions.
web.source_title: Gummy Stem Blight of Cucurbits | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Anthracnose: Check fruits for pink sporulation under high humidity. Leaf and stem lesions are smaller than Phytophthora blight lesions.
web.source_title: Facts in Depth: Phytophthora Blight | Vegetable Disease Facts - The Ohio State University

web.quote: Anthracnose is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Powdery mildew, caused by Podosphaera xanthii or Erysiphe cichoracearum, note white powdery growth on the leaf surface
web.source_title: Anthracnose of Cucurbits | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Fruit lesions appear as small water-soaked areas, similar to anthracnose, which rapidly enlarge to indefinite size.
web.source_title: Pythium (cottony leak) - Bugwoodwiki

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Bacterial wilt: Slowly pull apart fresh cut stem to look for sticky strands of bacteria. There is no root discoloration associated with bacterial wilt.
web.source_title: For the Professional Diagnostician — Phytophthora Blight (Cucurbit Diseases) | Vegetable Disease Facts, Ohio State University

web.quote: Target spot occurs sporadically and can be confused with downy mildew and other leaf spotting diseases.
web.source_title: Corynespora cassiicola (target spot of cucurbits) - Bugwoodwiki

web.quote: In the fall, target leaf spot on cucumber and watermelon could be confused with gummy stem blight, because symptoms of both diseases include leaf spots with target-like rings.
web.source_title: Field Scouting Guide: Gummy Stem Blight - Growing Produce (content/photos by Anthony Keinath, Clemson University extension plant pathologist)

web.quote: To distinguish gummy stem blight on watermelon from downy mildew, look at the size, shape, and position of leaf spots. Leaf spots of gummy stem blight are larger than individual spots of downy mildew.
web.source_title: Cucumber, Squash, Melon & Other Cucurbit Diseases | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson University

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Phytophthora leaf blight, caused by Phytophthora capsici
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Downy mildew and powdery mildew are diseases that may look similar at first glance, but are actually very different.
web.source_title: Mildew Diseases of Cucurbits | Integrated Pest Management | University of Connecticut

web.quote: Gummy stem blight or Plectosporium blight may cause stem lesions that could be confused with Phytophthora blight.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight of Cucurbits | Purdue University Vegetable Crops Hotline

web.quote: Fruit rot caused by Pythium species can be confused for that caused by Phytophthora capsici, another oomycete pathogen, because they both produce white growth on affected fruit.
web.source_title: Pythium fruit rot in Cucurbits | Vegetable Pathology – Long Island Horticultural Research & Extension Center, Cornell University

web.quote: In plants infected with Pythium, Phytophthora, or Rhizoctonia, the hypocotyl collapses and turns tan to brown, roots die, and cotyledons and/or leaves wilt.
web.source_title: Root Rots (Damping-Off) / Cucurbits / Agriculture: Pest Management Guidelines / UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: It is difficult to identify the pathogen that causes preemergence damping-off because the symptoms caused by Rhizoctonia are very similar to those for Pythium and Phytophthora.
web.source_title: Rhizoctonia Damping-off and Root Rot of Soybeans | Ohioline (Ohio State University Extension)

web.quote: These structures (sclerotia) are smaller and rounder than those of Botrytis neck rot.
web.source_title: Onions, Garlic: White rot | Hortsense | Washington State University

web.quote: Some of the browning and leaf drop reported recently, however, did not fit the typical symptoms associated with powdery mildew. Close examination of samples confirmed what I suspected: dogwood anthracnose disease, caused by the fungus Discula destructiva, was present, too, on about half of the samples in the "mini-survey"
web.source_title: Dramatic Dogwood Symptoms Draw Attention | Kentucky Pest News (University of Kentucky)

web.quote: Rhabdocline needle cast may be confused with Swiss needle cast.
web.source_title: Douglas Fir: Rhabdocline needle cast | Hortsense | Washington State University

web.quote: Phytophthora fruit rot in eggplant lacks the concentric patterns and dark fruiting structures present with Phomopsis rot, a fungus that also causes fruit rot on eggplant in Florida.
web.source_title: PP-176/VH045: Vegetable Diseases Caused by Phytophthora capsici in Florida (UF/IFAS Extension)

web.quote: Please note that twig blighting may look similar to flagging branches associated with Dutch elm disease (DED), so we recommend pruning off the branch and looking for internal vascular streaking to rule out DED.
web.source_title: Black Spot of Elm – Purdue Landscape Report

web.quote: Anthracnose and black rot are two common Iowa grape diseases. They both like the same environmental conditions, so often one plant will have both diseases, which can lead to confusion.
web.source_title: Grape Anthracnose and Black Rot | Yard and Garden, Iowa State University Extension

web.quote: Symptoms of sour rot can look similar to fungal bunch rot diseases, such as black rot (Ivey, 2016) shown in Figure 3, or Botrytis bunch rot (Ellis, 2016).
web.source_title: Sour Rot Disorder of Grape | Ohioline

web.quote: If growing abundantly on a leaf, downy mildew colonies can be confused with gray mold (Botrytis) or with powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew - Penn State Extension

web.quote: These symptoms are easily confused with downy mildew on grapes.
web.source_title: Powdery Mildew | Buckeye Appellation (Ohio State University CFAES)

web.quote: Other diseases of SHB, such as stem blight or Phomopsis twig blight, can cause symptoms similar to those of anthracnose during the same time of year.
web.source_title: PP337: Anthracnose on Southern Highbush Blueberry

web.quote: These symptoms can often be confused with other fungal diseases of highbush blueberry, including Phomopsis canker and twig blight and Botrytis blossom blight.
web.source_title: FS1361: Anthracnose Fruit Rot in Highbush Blueberry (Rutgers NJAES)

web.quote: Phomopsis twig blight may be difficult to distinguish from other diseases that can kill flower clusters, such as mummy berry, Botrytis, and anthracnose.
web.source_title: Be on the lookout for Phomopsis twig blight in blueberries - MSU Extension

web.quote: Phomopsis twig blight may be difficult to distinguish from other diseases that can kill flower clusters, such as mummy berry, Botrytis, and anthracnose.
web.source_title: Be on the lookout for Phomopsis twig blight in blueberries (MSU Extension)

hops__fusarium-wilts--blights--rots-and-.member_a: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: Hosta Virus X, Tobacco Rattle virus, and others ... Lab test required to distinguish between viruses and to verify infection
web.source_title: What's wrong with my plant? Hosta - Spots on leaves: Virus | University of Minnesota Extension

web.quote: Tip blight of junipers is caused by two different species of fungi, Phomopsis juniperovora and Kabatina juniperi, which cause similar symptoms in the affected plants.
web.source_title: Kabatina and Phomopsis Tip Blight | Yard and Garden, Iowa State University Extension and Outreach

web.quote: The fungus Diaporthe juniperivora (formerly Phomopsis juniperovora) is found most often in western Oregon.
web.source_title: Juniper (Juniperus spp.)-Twig Blight | Pacific Northwest Pest Management Handbooks

web.quote: Tip blight of junipers is caused by two different species of fungi, Phomopsis juniperovora and Kabatina juniperi, which cause similar symptoms in the affected plants.
web.source_title: Kabatina and Phomopsis Tip Blight | Yard and Garden | Iowa State University Extension and Outreach

web.quote: If growing abundantly on a leaf, downy mildew colonies can be confused with gray mold (Botrytis) or with powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew - Penn State Extension

web.quote: These downy growths initially may be confused with those of powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew on Lettuce / Home and Landscape / UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: Symptoms of Botrytis crown rot are similar to sclerotinia rot: lower leaves of plants infected with Botrytis yellow, and eventually the whole head wilts and rots.
web.source_title: Botrytis Rot (Gray Mold) on Lettuce — UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: Symptoms of Botrytis crown rot are similar to sclerotinia rot: lower leaves of plants infected with Botrytis yellow, and eventually the whole head wilts and rots. A watery, brown decay develops on the underside of the head at the crown. A dense gray or light brown sporulation appears over the damaged areas when moisture is abundant; this gray sporulation distinguishes Botrytis from lettuce drop.
web.source_title: Botrytis Rot (Gray Mold) on Lettuce / UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: The initial symptoms are similar to those caused by other basal stem rots (cottony rot, Rhizoctonia stem rot, etc.)
web.source_title: Southern Blight / Floriculture and Ornamental Nurseries / Agriculture: Pest Management Guidelines / UC Statewide IPM Program (UC IPM)

web.quote: Sclerotinia diseases on vegetable crops are commonly referred to as white mold but may also be known as timber rot (tomato), drop (lettuce), and head rot (cabbage).
web.source_title: Sclerotinia Diseases of Vegetables (PPFS-VG-29), University of Kentucky College of Agriculture, Plant Pathology Extension

web.quote: Sclerotinia stem rots may be confused with those caused by the fungus Sclerotium.
web.source_title: Plant Disease Library: Sclerotinia — University of Georgia Department of Plant Pathology Extension & Outreach

web.quote: Sclerotinia stem rots may be confused with those caused by the fungus Sclerotium. The disease common name, "white mold," has been used to describe diseases caused by both fungi.
web.source_title: Plant Pathology: Extension & Outreach: Plant Disease Library — Sclerotinia (University of Georgia)

web.quote: The lesions are very dark in color, so one might confuse the lesions with those caused by tar spot (black tar spot lesions develop in late summer or early fall, so we have that to look forward to).
web.source_title: Maple anthracnose - Michigan State University Extension

web.quote: Other foliar diseases that could be confused with downy mildew when scouting include angular leaf spot, anthracnose, Alternaria leaf blight, and powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew Update: July 15, 2025 - Penn State Extension

melon__alternaria-leaf-blight__fusarium-.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: Lesions of Alternaria leaf spot can often be confused with young gummy stem blight leaf spots because of the zonate appearance.
web.source_title: PDMG-V3-55/PG060: 2024 Florida Plant Disease Management Guide: Watermelon

web.quote: On mature watermelon foliage, distinct dark to reddish-brown lesions develop along leaf veins; however, these may be inconspicuous and indistinguishable from symptoms of other foliar diseases (e.g., anthracnose and gummy stem blight).
web.source_title: Bacterial fruit blotch of cucurbits — APS (The American Phytopathological Society)

web.quote: Anthracnose and downy mildew provide similar disease symptoms in watermelons.
web.source_title: Under the Microscope: Melon Growers Should Consult Extension When Diagnosing Disease

melon__anthracnose__fusarium-wilts--blig.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: Young GSB lesions can be difficult to visually distinguish from lesions of anthracnose and target leaf spot.

web.quote: The bacterium causes small, angular or linear necrotic lesions on true leaves. These lesions are small and similar to those caused by downy mildew or gummy stem blight.
web.source_title: 2024 Florida Plant Disease Management Guide: Watermelon (PDMG-V3-55/PG060), UF/IFAS Extension

melon__bacterial-fruit-blotch__fusarium-.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: Lesions on mature leaves are reddish brown and elongated along the leaf veins, but they are easily confused with other diseases like gummy stem blight and anthracnose.
web.source_title: Diseases of Cucurbit Seedlings | Weekly Crop Update — University of Delaware Cooperative Extension

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Bacterial fruit blotch: No white-tan, velvety sporulation will be observed on the fruit.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight (Advanced) — Cucurbit Diseases | Vegetable Disease Facts, The Ohio State University

web.quote: Bacterial wilt symptoms are easily confused with aboveground symptoms of root rots and other vascular diseases, so isolation of the pathogen followed by phenotypic identification, PCR, or other molecular assay is necessary to confirm a diagnosis.
web.source_title: Bacterial Wilt of Cucurbits: Resurrecting a Classic Pathosystem | Plant Disease (APS)

web.quote: If your cucurbit crops are wilting, it could be cucurbit yellow vine decline, Fusarium, or bacterial wilt that is causing the problem.
web.source_title: Why Are My Cucurbits Wilting? - Penn State Extension

melon__bacterial-wilt__fusarium-wilts--b.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Bacterial wilt: Slowly pull apart fresh cut stem to look for sticky strands of bacteria. There is no root discoloration associated with bacterial wilt.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight (Advanced) — Vegetable Disease Facts, Ohio State University

web.quote: In the desert melon production areas of Arizona and California, symptoms of plant wilting and collapse usually can be attributed to one of four diseases; Charcoal rot, Fusarium wilt, Monosporascus root rot, or Pythium sudden wilt. Each disease is caused by a different soil-borne plant pathogen, so knowing what management options might be available first requires accurate identification of the responsible pathogen.
web.source_title: Sudden Wilt and Death of Melon Plants — Vegetable IPM Updates, Dr. Mike Matheron, University of Arizona Cooperative Extension (Yuma Agricultural Center)

melon__charcoal-rot__fusarium-wilts--bli.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: These symptoms, plus the resin-like drops on the stem might look like gummy stem blight. However, a look under the microscope revealed fungal structures and spores that were not from the gummy stem blight fungus.
web.source_title: New Disease Report – Charcoal Rot of Cucumber – Purdue University Vegetable Crops Hotline

web.quote: To distinguish gummy stem blight on watermelon from downy mildew, look at the size, shape, and position of leaf spots. Leaf spots of gummy stem blight are larger than individual spots of downy mildew.
web.source_title: Cucumber, Squash, Melon & Other Cucurbit Diseases | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson Cooperative Extension

web.quote: If answered yes to questions 1 and 2, take a moment to rule out these other diseases before confirming your CDM diagnosis.
web.source_title: Other Diseases - Cucurbit Downy Mildew ipmPIPE Diagnostic Key

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Powdery mildew, caused by Podosphaera xanthii or Erysiphe cichoracearum, note white powdery growth on the leaf surface

web.quote: Although this symptom gives gummy stem blight its common name, this symptom is not diagnostic; stem injuries and Fusarium wilt also cause gumming.

Each disease is caused by a different soil-borne plant pathogen, so knowing what management options might be available first requires accurate identification of the responsible pathogen.

web.quote: Gummy stem blight or Plectosporium blight may cause stem lesions that could be confused with Phytophthora blight.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight of Cucurbit Crops | Scouting Guides for Problems of Vegetables, University of Kentucky
Monosporascus cannonballus (NC State University, PP728)

web.quote: People often confuse symptoms of Tubakia leaf spot with those of oak anthracnose.
web.source_title: Tubakia (Actinopelte) Leaf Spot – Wisconsin Horticulture

web.quote: Other diseases, such as some wood decays and anthracnose, might be confused with oak wilt symptoms.
web.source_title: Oak Wilt | Ohioline - The Ohio State University Extension

web.quote: Indeed, BLS symptoms are also often confused with those of Oak wilt and Tubakia leaf spot.
web.source_title: Bacterial Leaf Scorch — MU Plant Diagnostic Clinic, University of Missouri Extension

web.quote: Leaf scorch and premature leaf drop caused by Xf are similar to symptoms that can be caused by Oak wilt and Tubakia.
web.source_title: Bacterial Leaf Scorch of Oak: Lab Confirmation Necessary

web.quote: Many inquiries with concerns of oak wilt instead show the symptoms and symptom distribution in the tree that match Botryosphaeria twig canker.
web.source_title: Oak Wilt or Bot Twig Canker? | Yard and Garden, Iowa State University Extension and Outreach

web.quote: Symptoms of oak wilt are easily confused with other problems including gas leaks, Ganoderma root rot, and Hypoxylon canker.
web.source_title: Problem: Oak Wilt - Ceratocystis fagacearum (Kansas State University Cooperative Extension Service)

web.quote: Heavily congregated Tubakia leaf spots are often confused as Oak wilt or Anthracnose disease.
web.source_title: Tubakia Leaf Spot on Oak | University of Missouri Extension Plant Diagnostic Clinic

web.quote: Young lesions may be confused with those of brown rot caused by Monilinia species, and Botryosphaeria species (black rot and white rot). At this stage, identification depends on laboratory isolations.
web.source_title: Peach Disease - Anthracnose (Penn State Extension)

web.quote: Similarities in spot color on some peanut cultivars can make early and late leaf spot difficult to distinguish.
web.source_title: Foliar Diseases of Peanuts - Alabama Cooperative Extension System

web.quote: Botrytis (Gray Mold) and anthracnose often cause similar damage. Culturing in a lab is usually necessary for proper identification.
web.source_title: Peony diseases (Paeona sp.)-Hort Answers - University of Illinois Extension

web.quote: Some symptoms can mimic those caused by bacterial and fungal pathogens, or chemical injury.
web.source_title: Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus (TSWV): a virus you should know and be able to identify! | UNH Extension

web.quote: It sometimes is confused with powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Phlox-Downy Mildew | Pacific Northwest Pest Management Handbooks

plum-and-cherry__black-knot__blumeriella.member_b: Blumeriella Leaf Spot Of Cherry And Plum

plum-and-cherry__blumeriella-leaf-spot-o.member_a: Blumeriella Leaf Spot Of Cherry And Plum

web.quote: Cherry Leaf Spot (Shothole), caused by Blumeriella jaapii, (formerly known as Coccomyces hiemalis) anamorph Phloeosporella padi, is the most important fungal disease of cherry wherever cherries are grown.
web.source_title: Cherry Leaf Spot - University of Arkansas Cooperative Extension Service, Plant Health Clinic Disease Notes

plum-and-cherry__blumeriella-leaf-spot-o.member_a: Blumeriella Leaf Spot Of Cherry And Plum

web.quote: Infections of other tuber rot organisms such as soft rot bacteria, pink rot or leak frequently may use late blight-affected areas as infection courts and often progress more rapidly than P. infestans, making diagnosis difficult.
web.source_title: Late Blight in Potato | NDSU Agriculture

web.quote: However, infected tissues are easily invaded by secondary pathogens, such as soft rot bacteria (Pectobacterium spp.), which produce the slimly symptoms often found in potatoes with pink rot
web.source_title: Potato Diseases: Pink Rot - MSU Extension (Bulletin E2993)

web.quote: Poor stands may also be mistaken for seed tuber decay caused by Fusarium or soft rot bacteria unless plants are dug up and examined.
web.source_title: Potato Diseases: Rhizoctonia Stem Canker and Black Scurf - MSU Extension

web.quote: Severely affected plants may have severe defoliation that resembles late blight.
web.source_title: Potato (Solanum tuberosum)-Tomato Spotted Wilt | Pacific Northwest Pest Management Handbooks

web.quote: You can submit samples to diagnostic labs (e.g., UW–Madison Plant Disease Diagnostics) in order to differentiate the plant damage from Pythium root rot, Fusarium root rot, and/or Phytophthora root rot.
web.source_title: Rhizoctonia – UW Vegetable Pathology – UW–Madison

web.quote: Another common fungal disease on red pine is Sirococcus shoot blight, although it isn't as widespread or as severe as Diplodia in Minnesota. It cannot be distinguished from Diplodia without a microscope.
web.source_title: Diplodia identification | Minnesota DNR

web.quote: Symptoms can appear very similar to symptoms of bacterial blight and soybean rust, so knowledge of the differences between these diseases is critical for accurate diagnosis.
web.source_title: Bacterial Blight and Bacterial Pustule (Extended Version) | OSU Soybean Diseases

web.quote: Symptoms can be confused with relatively minor diseases such as brown leaf spot, Phyllosticta leaf spot, bacterial pustule, and bacterial blight, as well as herbicide damage, viruses, and other issues.
web.source_title: Spray Decisions for Frogeye Leaf Spot in Soybeans - farmdoc (University of Illinois Extension)

web.quote: Bacterial blight can be mistaken for Septoria brown spot. The two diseases can be distinguished by the presence of a halo around bacterial blight lesions.
web.source_title: Bacterial blight on soybean | UMN Extension

web.quote: Another common soybean rust look-a-like is bacterial blight. This disease is caused by the bacterium Pseudomonas savastanoi pv. glycinea, which causes small, angular leaf spots with yellow haloes.
web.source_title: Soybean Rust Look-a-likes (Hudelson), University of Wisconsin-Madison/Extension Plant Disease Diagnostics Clinic, Proc. of the 2006 Wisconsin Fertilizer, Aglime & Pest Management Conference

web.quote: Several diseases produce symptoms that can be confused with those caused by SVNV.
web.source_title: An Overview of Soybean Vein Necrosis Virus — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Can be confused with Septoria brown spot, powdery mildew and bacterial diseases
web.source_title: Soybean Disease Diagnostic Series | NDSU Agriculture

web.quote: Symptoms can be confused with relatively minor diseases such as brown leaf spot, Phyllosticta leaf spot, bacterial pustule, and bacterial blight, as well as herbicide damage, viruses, and other issues.
web.source_title: Spray Decisions for Frogeye Leaf Spot in Soybeans - farmdoc, University of Illinois

web.quote: Commonly confused with bacterial blight or Septoria brown spot
web.source_title: Soybean Disease Diagnostic Series | NDSU Agriculture

web.quote: This disease can easily be confused with soybean rust.
web.source_title: Bacterial Pustule - CropWatch, University of Nebraska-Lincoln (Loren J. Giesler, Extension Plant Pathologist)

web.quote: Several diseases produce symptoms that can be confused with those caused by SVNV. Accurate diagnosis of SVNV is essential to avoid unnecessary management actions that are ineffective against this disease.
web.source_title: An Overview of Soybean Vein Necrosis Virus — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Bean pod mottle virus is sporadic in Minnesota and North Dakota, unlikely to cause yield loss, and commonly confused with bacterial blight or Septoria brown spot.
web.source_title: Soybean Disease Diagnostic Series | NDSU Agriculture (Extension)

web.quote: Cercospora leaf blight can be confused with other diseases and disorders. ... Frogeye leaf spot (Cercospora sojina) – Frogeye leaf spot (FLS) is characterized by the small gray lesions with reddish-brown borders on upper leaf surfaces. ... The foliar phase of FLS is usually severe and easily distinguishable from CLB when FLS petiole lesions occur.
web.source_title: An Overview of Cercospora Leaf Blight of Soybean — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Young SVNV lesions that have not developed distinct margins can be easily confused with Cercospora leaf blight.
web.source_title: An Overview of Soybean Vein Necrosis Virus

web.quote: FICTION: Soybean stem zone lines are associated with the stem disease charcoal rot, caused by the fungus Macrophomina phaseolina. However, this association of soybean stem zone lines and charcoal rot is incorrect and has persisted for several decades, leading to frequent misdiagnosis of the disorder. ... The key takeaway is that zone lines on the interior of soybean stems are associated with Diaporthe fungi and not the fungus that causes charcoal rot, as previously reported.
web.source_title: Soybean Stem Zone Lines: Fact and Fiction — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: How to distinguish PRR from charcoal rot: Stems of Phytophthora-infected plants have characteristic dark brown lesions visible on the outer stem tissue that are continuous from the roots and up the lower stem.
web.source_title: An Overview of Charcoal Rot of Soybean
An Overview of Charcoal Rot of Soybean (Crop Protection Network)

web.quote: Infected seed has a dull white appearance and is partially or completely covered with a pale coating of fungal spores which can be confused with white mold.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew - Soybean Disease - Soybean Research & Information Network (SRIN), authored by Douglas J. Jardine, Professor Emeritus, Kansas State University

web.quote: Older lesions turn grayish-brown-to-dark brown with yellowish-green margins and may finally become entirely brown and have an appearance similar to other foliar diseases such as Septoria brown spot and soybean rust.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew - UT Crops Pest Guides

web.quote: Other diseases of soybean including brown spot [see UW Plant Disease Facts, Brown Spot (Septoria Leaf Spot)], bacterial pustule and particularly downy mildew could potentially be confused with soybean rust.
web.source_title: Soybean Rust | Plant Disease Diagnostics Clinic, University of Wisconsin-Madison

web.quote: Several diseases produce symptoms that can be confused with those caused by SVNV.
web.source_title: An Overview of Soybean Vein Necrosis Virus — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Young leaves are most susceptible, which helps differentiate this disease from Septoria brown spot, which tends to infect older leaves in the lower canopy.
web.source_title: Frogeye Leaf Spot on Soybean | Cooperative Extension | University of Delaware

web.quote: Stem canker (caused by Diaporthe caulivora) is the disease most commonly confused with Phytophthora root and stem rot.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Root and Stem Rot of Soybean | NDSU Agriculture

web.quote: Northern stem canker can also be confused with white mold when diagnosing above the canopy. Because the lesions can girdle stems, leaf flagging and death can resemble that of white mold damage.
web.source_title: Stem Canker Prevalent in Wisconsin Soybean Fields — University of Wisconsin-Madison Integrated Pest and Crop Management

web.quote: stem canker lesions generally occur on one side of the stem, while the lesion resulting from southern blight will girdle the entire stem
web.source_title: Sudden Death Syndrome Look Alike Part II: Southern Blight of Soybean (Tom Allen, Extension Plant Pathologist, Mississippi Crop Situation)

web.quote: Foliar symptoms of TRD include interveinal chlorosis and necrosis of leaves, which can be confused with other diseases such as red crown rot, stem canker, and sudden death syndrome.
web.source_title: Taproot Decline of Soybean - Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Foliar symptoms of Sclerotinia stem rot could be mistaken for late-season symptoms of brown stem rot, Fusarium wilt, Phytophthora root rot, and stem canker.
web.source_title: Information on Sclerotinia Stem Rot, Laboratory for Soybean Disease Research, University of Illinois

web.quote: The southern blight disease of tomato is caused by Sclerotium rolfsii, which is very easy to be confused with white mold disease.
web.source_title: White Mold Disease // Missouri Environment and Garden News Article // Integrated Pest Management, University of Missouri

web.quote: This disease is easily confused with soybean rust.
web.source_title: Brown Spot of Soybean (University of Nebraska-Lincoln Extension, G2059)

web.quote: How to distinguish brown spot from SVNV: Brown spot lesions are mainly located in the lower canopy and are not associated with leaf veins like SVNV lesions.
web.source_title: An Overview of Soybean Vein Necrosis Virus — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: The foliar symptoms of TRD can be confused with many other soybean maladies, including chemical phytotoxicity, which is typically from the demethylation inhibitors (DMI, triazole); red crown rot; decline due to root-knot nematodes; southern blight; stem canker; and sudden death syndrome.
web.source_title: Louisiana Plant Pathology Disease Identification and Management Series: Taproot Decline of Soybean (Xylaria necrophora)

web.quote: White Rust (fungus – Albugo occidentalis): White rust is the major disease of spinach in Texas. ... Downy Mildew or Blue Mold (fungus – Peronospora effusa): The downy mildew fungus first causes yellowish areas on the upper side of the leaf.
web.source_title: Spinach (Spinacia oleracea) — Texas Plant Disease Handbook, Texas A&M AgriLife Extension

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: Anthracnose, caused by Colletotrichum orbiculare
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: On watermelon, gummy stem blight can be distinguished from anthracnose by the larger leaf spots, larger lesions that completely encircle petioles, and the presence of crown cankers, which do not form with anthracnose.
web.source_title: Gummy Stem Blight on Watermelon and Muskmelon

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Anthracnose: Check fruits for pink sporulation under high humidity. Leaf and stem lesions are smaller than Phytophthora blight lesions.
web.source_title: Facts in Depth | Vegetable Disease Facts — Cucurbit Diseases: Phytophthora Blight (Ohio State University)

web.quote: If answered yes to questions 1 and 2, take a moment to rule out these other diseases before confirming your CDM diagnosis. ... Gummy stem blight leaf lesions are dark brown and often develop near margins. Gummy stem blight can be differentiated from CDM because it produces ovate stem and vine cankers near the nodes, with a characteristic brown gummy exudate.
web.source_title: Other Diseases - Cucurbit Downy Mildew ipmPIPE Diagnostic Key

web.quote: Phytophthora tends to thrive in hot weather and downy mildew in cooler weather, but there is considerable overlap.
web.source_title: Managing Cucumber Downy Mildew and Phytophthora Blight (Ohio Veggie Disease News, OSU)
web.claim: Co-scouted cucurbit blights; both blight foliage/plant and are managed together, with overlapping conditions.

web.quote: Powdery mildew is commonly mistaken for downy mildew, yet the characteristic patterns of necrosis and chlorosis on the upper leaf surface are absent and instead replaced by a light 'powder' of spores.
web.source_title: Downy Mildew | Cornell Vegetables (Cornell University)

web.quote: Gummy stem blight or Plectosporium blight may cause stem lesions that could be confused with Phytophthora blight.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight of Cucurbits | Purdue University Vegetable Crops Hotline

web.quote: Gummy stem blight or Plectosporium blight may cause stem lesions that could be confused with Phytophthora blight.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight of Cucurbits | Purdue University Vegetable Crops Hotline

web.quote: Southern blight symptoms resemble Sclerotinia white mold, Phytophthora crown rot, Verticillium wilt and Fusarium wilt.
web.source_title: Field Scouting Guide: Southern Blight - Growing Produce

web.quote: Botrytis gray mold, caused by a fungus, can also turn caps brown.
web.source_title: Strawberry Disease - Angular Leaf Spot (Penn State Extension)

strawberry__angular-leaf-spot-of-strawbe.member_b: Phomopsis Leaf Blight And Fruit Rot

web.quote: On the upper leaf surface, the lesions look like rather nondescript, reddish-brown spots and could easily be mistaken for fungal leaf spots.
web.source_title: Watch out for angular leaf spot in strawberries — Michigan State University Extension

web.quote: Berry flowers may be blighted, though this is difficult to tell apart from blossoms blighted by botrytis.
web.source_title: Anthracnose on Strawberry Fruit

web.quote: In later stages of development, fruit rot symptoms resemble that of anthracnose fruit rot.
web.source_title: Phomopsis Leaf Blight and Fruit Rot of Strawberry | Ohioline

strawberry__anthracnose__phomopsis-leaf-.member_b: Phomopsis Leaf Blight And Fruit Rot

strawberry__gray-mold__phomopsis-leaf-bl.member_b: Phomopsis Leaf Blight And Fruit Rot

web.quote: Symptoms can be mistaken for common leaf spot, caused by M. fragariae.
web.source_title: First Report of Botrytis cinerea Causing Leaf Spot on Strawberry in Florida (Plant Disease, APS)

web.quote: During the early stages, symptoms are practically indistinguishable from common leaf spot lesions, caused by M. fragariae
web.source_title: PP359: Leaf Spot Diseases of Strawberry

strawberry__phomopsis-leaf-blight-and-fr.member_a: Phomopsis Leaf Blight And Fruit Rot

web.quote: Secondary symptoms vary depending on the severity of infection, and may include stunting, wilting, yellowing, and dropping of leaves, much as with Fusarium wilt (see below).
web.source_title: Irish Potato & Sweetpotato Diseases | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson Cooperative Extension

web.quote: Several other soil-borne, fungal diseases of Okinawan sweetpotatoes that occur in Hawai'i may be confused with the initial symptoms of Java black rot. They may occur together on the same sweetpotatoes infected by D. gossypina, or even in the same lesions. The similar diseases are Ceratocystis black rot, Rhizopus soft rot, and diseases caused by Fusarium sp.
web.source_title: Java Black Rot of Okinawan Sweetpotato (UH-CTAHR Plant Disease PD-55)

web.quote: Several other soil-borne, fungal diseases of Okinawan sweetpotatoes that occur in Hawai'i may be confused with the initial symptoms of Java black rot. They may occur together on the same sweetpotatoes infected by D. gossypina, or even in the same lesions. The similar diseases are Ceratocystis black rot, Rhizopus soft rot, and diseases caused by Fusarium sp.
web.source_title: Java Black Rot of Okinawan Sweetpotato (PD-55), University of Hawaii CTAHR Cooperative Extension Service

web.quote: Sweetpotato scurf can resemble black rot lesions, however, black rot lesions tend to have a black center due to pathogen sporulation and lesions can go into the sweetpotato flesh.
web.source_title: How to Identify and Manage Sweetpotato Scurf Caused by Monilochaetes Infuscans | NC State Extension

web.quote: bread mold (Rhizopus stolonifer (Ehrenb.) Vuill.)
web.source_title: bread mold (Rhizopus stolonifer (Ehrenb.) Vuill.) on sweetpotato - IPM Images / Bugwood (University of Georgia)

web.quote: Severely affected plants can have vascular discoloration, which may be confused with Fusarium wilt.
web.source_title: Diagnostic Guide for Sclerotial Blight and Circular Spot of Sweetpotato | Plant Health Progress
Irish Potato & Sweetpotato Diseases | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson Cooperative Extension

web.quote: They may occur together on the same sweetpotatoes infected by D. gossypina, or even in the same lesions. The similar diseases are Ceratocystis black rot, Rhizopus soft rot, and diseases caused by Fusarium sp.
web.source_title: Java Black Rot of Okinawan Sweetpotato (PD-55), University of Hawaii at Manoa, College of Tropical Agriculture and Human Resources (CTAHR)

web.quote: Lesions can easily be confused with those of Streptomyces soil rot, but differ in that the margins of the lesions are usually sharply defined and circular, there is no identation of the root, the lesion is usually not cracked but may begin to peel off shortly after harvest, and the tissue is a lighter yellowish brown.
web.source_title: Sclerotial Blight and Circular Spot | LSU AgCenter

tomato__anthracnose__bacterial-canker-an.member_b: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: If you see lesions on the fruit, you're likely dealing with anthracnose. If lesions are primarily on lower leaves with characteristic gray centers, it's likely septoria leaf spot.
web.source_title: Tomato Diseases & Disorders (Clemson HGIC)
web.claim: Commonly confused tomato diseases; separated by fruit-vs-leaf involvement and gray-centered leaf spots.

web.quote: The lesions become soft and sunken and at this stage may look like early stages of fruit infection due to Anthracnose disease.
web.source_title: Leaf mold — Tomato, Plant Pathology, University of Florida (UF/IFAS u-scout)

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Bacterial Speck – Look for small black pin-point lesions on the fruit with no white halo as indicators of bacterial speck.
web.source_title: For the Professional Diagnostician | Bacterial Canker | Vegetable Disease Facts - Ohio State University

tomato__bacterial-canker-and-wilt-of-tom.member_a: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: Bacterial canker is caused by Corynebacterium michiganensis, and plants affected early in their development wilt and die and the disease may be confused with bacterial wilt.
web.source_title: Common Diseases of Tomatoes- Part II: Diseases Caused by Bacteria, Viruses and Nematodes (EPP-7626) | Oklahoma State University Extension

tomato__bacterial-canker-and-wilt-of-tom.member_a: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: Often Confused With ... Early Blight – Look for lesions with concentric rings and chlorosis as indicators of early blight.
web.source_title: For the Professional Diagnostician | Vegetable Disease Facts (Ohio State University) — Tomato Bacterial Canker

tomato__bacterial-canker-and-wilt-of-tom.member_a: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: There are several diseases and disorders with symptoms that could be mistaken for late blight. ... Sometimes canker causes large areas of leaf tissue to die without the characteristic yellow border for this disease. Affected tissue always extends from the leaf margin whereas late blight can cause spots in the center part of leaves.
web.source_title: Late blight 'imitators' | Long Island Vegetable Health (Cornell University)

tomato__bacterial-canker-and-wilt-of-tom.member_a: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: Plants affected early in their development wilt and die and the disease may be confused with bacterial wilt.
web.source_title: Common Diseases of Tomatoes- Part II: Diseases Caused by Bacteria, Viruses and Nematodes (EPP-7626) | Oklahoma State University Extension

tomato__bacterial-canker-and-wilt-of-tom.member_a: Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato
Bacterial Canker And Wilt Of Tomato

web.quote: Bacterial speck and spot might be confused with other common diseases. Early blight leaf spots are generally larger (1/2″), have a target appearance, and may have a light-colored ring around each spot.
web.source_title: Bacterial Speck and Spot of Tomato | Integrated Pest Management, University of Connecticut

web.quote: Bacterial spot or speck can also be easily confused with Septoria.
web.source_title: Scouting and Identifying Tomato Diseases - Penn State Extension

web.quote: Leaf symptoms of bacterial speck can be hard to distinguish from other tomato diseases. Bacterial spot, (see UW Plant Disease Facts D0012, Bacterial Spot of Tomato) and tomato spotted wilt (a viral disease) may cause similar leaf symptoms. Laboratory testing may be needed to determine which disease is affecting your tomatoes.
web.source_title: Bacterial Speck of Tomato – Wisconsin Horticulture (University of Wisconsin-Madison Extension)

web.quote: Over time the entire plant will slowly collapse looking similar to a wilt or root rot pathogen as the water supply is cut off to the plant.
web.source_title: Identifying white mold in tomato production — Rutgers Plant & Pest Advisory
Bacterial Wilt of Tomato | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Bacterial wilt or Southern bacterial blight is a serious disease caused by Ralstonia solanacearum (formerly Pseudomonas solanacearum).
web.source_title: Tomato Diseases & Disorders | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson Cooperative Extension

web.quote: Look alike diseases: Bacterial wilt on tomato may be confused with other wilt or root rot diseases. Southern blight on tomato will also wilt, but affected plants will have a crown rot with white strands or mycelium growing up the stem from the soil line.
web.source_title: Bacterial Wilt of Tomatoes | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Look alike diseases ... Bacterial wilt on tomato may be confused with other wilt or root rot diseases. ... Verticillium wilt typically causes V-shaped lesions on the leaflets and does not wilt as suddenly.
web.source_title: Bacterial Wilt of Tomatoes | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Do not confuse early blight with two other diseases, late blight and Septoria leaf spot.
web.source_title: Early Blight of Tomatoes | Extension | West Virginia University

web.quote: Septoria leaf spot may be confused with early blight, which is caused by Alternaria solani.
web.source_title: Septoria Leaf Spot | Extension | West Virginia University

web.quote: Defoliation progresses from the base of the plant upwards and resembles early blight from a distance.
web.source_title: Common Diseases of Tomatoes: Part I. Diseases Caused by Fungi - Oklahoma State University Extension

web.quote: Symptoms of TSWV may also resemble symptoms of early blight or other leaf spot diseases; however, lesions caused by TSWV have blurred margins, while the margins of lesions caused by other diseases are definite.
web.source_title: Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus on Tomato and Pepper | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: The spots may be confused with alternaria early blight, but they are not definite, nor do they develop concentric bull's-eye rings.
web.source_title: Verticillium Wilt of Tomato — Cornell Cooperative Extension / NYS IPM (Diseases of Tomatoes, by Arden Sherf, Dept. of Plant Pathology, Cornell University)

web.quote: This is the disease most commonly confused for late blight because the pathogen causes large leaf spots, stem lesions, and affects fruit.
web.source_title: Late blight 'imitators' — Cornell University Vegetable Pathology (livegpath)

web.quote: The gray fuzzy growth of the gray mold pathogen distinguishes this from other diseases, in particular late blight, and from secondary fungi that decompose dead tissue.
web.source_title: Gray mold on tomatoes — Cornell Long Island Vegetable Health (livegpath)

web.quote: Mycelial growth is black and very fuzzy, resembling that produced by Botrytis cinerea. Severe infection on the fruit becomes rotted, but unlike Botrytis grey mold, the fruit also becomes tough, dried, and mummified.
web.source_title: Facts in Depth | Botrytis Gray Mold — Vegetable Disease Facts (Ohio State University Extension)

web.quote: Sclerotinia white mold – Infected stems become bleached and are covered with a very fuzzy white mycelium. This mycelium can spread to flowers and other parts of the plant, similar to Botrytis gray mold.
web.source_title: Facts in Depth | Vegetable Disease Facts — Botrytis Gray Mold (Advanced), Ohio State University Extension

web.quote: Leaf mold may be confused with Botrytis gray mold, late blight, and powdery mildew.
web.source_title: Foliar Fungal Diseases on High Tunnel and Greenhouse Tomatoes | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Tomato spotted wilt virus causes a ring pattern that can be confused with early blight, gray mold, or other fungal diseases.
web.source_title: Field Scouting Guide: How To Identify Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus - Growing Produce

web.quote: Other species of Phytophthora, specifically P. nicotianae and P. capsici, can also cause lesions on tomato and tomato fruit similar to those caused by late blight.
web.source_title: Tomato Late Blight | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Late blight: Large yellowish-brown lesions with irregular margins for on the foliage of tomato plants. Coppery-colored lesions on fruit are similar to fruit symptoms caused by P. fulva.
web.source_title: Diagnosticians | High Tunnel Disease Facts (Ohio State University) — Tomato Leaf Mold, "Often Confused With"

web.quote: Other tomato and potato diseases such as Septoria leaf spot (see UW Plant Disease Facts D0100, Septoria Leaf Spot), early blight (see UW Plant Disease Facts D0046, Early Blight) and Verticillium wilt (see UW Plant Disease Facts D0122, Verticillium Wilt of Vegetables) may be misidentified as late blight.
web.source_title: Late Blight – Wisconsin Horticulture (UW Plant Disease Facts D0068), University of Wisconsin-Madison Division of Extension

web.quote: Not all 'leaf blights' of tomato and potato are late blight! Several common diseases of tomato and potato cause leaf symptoms that can easily be confused with late blight. These include Fulvia leaf mold, early blight, Septoria leaf spot, and bacterial diseases.
web.source_title: Solanaceous, Late Blight — UMass Amherst Center for Agriculture, Food, and the Environment (Vegetable Fact Sheet)
Bacterial Wilt of Tomatoes | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: The southern blight disease of tomato is caused by Sclerotium rolfsii, which is very easy to be confused with white mold disease.
web.source_title: White Mold Disease // Missouri Environment and Garden News Article // Integrated Pest Management, University of Missouri

web.quote: Over time the entire plant will slowly collapse looking similar to a wilt or root rot pathogen as the water supply is cut off to the plant.
web.source_title: Identifying white mold in greenhouse & high tunnel tomato production — Rutgers Plant & Pest Advisory

web.quote: Wilting tomatoes are a common complaint. There are several possible causes for this (other than lack of water), but the most common are probably southern bacterial wilt and southern blight. Both of these can cause wilting and plant death without leaf spots or plant stunting. In spite of the similarity in the names, these diseases are caused by different pathogens – one bacterial and one fungal.
web.source_title: It's Time to Talk About Wilting Tomatoes — LSU AgCenter

web.quote: Lack of moisture or symptoms of other wilt diseases including bacterial wilt, Fusarium wilt, and southern blight may be confused with symptoms of Verticillium wilt.
web.source_title: Verticillium Wilt of Tomato and Eggplant | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Southern blight symptoms may be confused with symptoms of Verticillium wilt, but V-shaped leaf lesions are distinctive of Verticillium wilt.
web.source_title: Common Diseases of Tomatoes: Part I. Diseases Caused by Fungi - Oklahoma State University Extension

web.quote: The plant may look as if it has a vascular wilt.
web.source_title: Tomato Spotted Wilt Virus | Integrated Pest Management | University of Connecticut

web.quote: In the field, leaf lesions are often difficult to find and may be confused with lesions caused by pathogenic fungi.
web.source_title: Bacterial Fruit Blotch - Watermelon Foliar Diseases | Aggie Horticulture, Texas A&M University

web.quote: Anthracnose and downy mildew provide similar disease symptoms in watermelons.
web.source_title: Under the Microscope: Melon Growers Should Consult Extension When Diagnosing Disease

web.quote: On watermelon, gummy stem blight can be distinguished from anthracnose by the larger leaf spots, larger lesions that completely encircle petioles, and the presence of crown cankers, which do not form with anthracnose.
web.source_title: Gummy Stem Blight on Watermelon and Muskmelon | Land-Grant Press | Clemson University, South Carolina

web.quote: Lesions on mature leaves are reddish brown and elongated along the leaf veins, but they are easily confused with other diseases like gummy stem blight and anthracnose.
web.source_title: Diseases of Cucurbit Seedlings | Weekly Crop Update (University of Delaware Cooperative Extension)

web.quote: Initial symptoms of bacterial fruit blotch of watermelon are similar to those caused by P. capsici. However, after lesions expand, the two diseases can be easily separated because of the presence of extensive rind cracking, absence of fungal-like growth, and other symptoms associated with bacterial fruit blotch.
web.source_title: PP-176/VH045: Vegetable Diseases Caused by Phytophthora capsici in Florida (UF/IFAS Extension)

web.quote: To distinguish gummy stem blight on watermelon from downy mildew, look at the size, shape, and position of leaf spots. Leaf spots of gummy stem blight are larger than individual spots of downy mildew. Some leaf spots of gummy stem blight have a ringed or target look.
web.source_title: Cucumber, Squash, Melon & Other Cucurbit Diseases | Home & Garden Information Center, Clemson University

web.quote: Downy mildew is often confused with other foliar diseases such as: ... Phytophthora leaf blight, caused by Phytophthora capsici
web.source_title: Cucurbit Downy Mildew | NC State Extension Publications

web.quote: Keeping symptoms straight can be confusing, and keeping names straight adds another layer of deduction. Downy mildew or powdery mildew—while both are called "mildew," there are differences in how they look on your plants and how you treat the disease.
web.source_title: Powdery Mildew vs Downy Mildew – Gardening Solutions, University of Florida IFAS

web.quote: Phytophthora blight primarily affects watermelon fruits, whereas gummy stem blight affects both leaves and stems.
web.source_title: Disease Watch: Gummy Stem Blight, Anthracnose, and Phytophthora in Cucurbits (Purdue Vegetable Crops Hotline)
web.claim: Co-discussed cucurbit blights; separated by foliage/stem (GSB) vs fruit (Phytophthora) involvement.

web.quote: For example, Pythium may also cause damping-off; often it is not easy to tell the difference between damping-off caused by Pythium and Phytophthora blight.
web.source_title: Phytophthora Blight of Cucurbits | Purdue University Vegetable Crops Hotline

web.quote: Initial symptoms of powdery mildew appear on the older leaves with a reddish-brown, bronzed appearance. At this point, other problems such as viral infections, chemical phytotoxicity, and certain nutrient deficiencies can cause similar symptoms; therefore, microscopic examination may be needed to observe the causal agent.
web.source_title: Powdery Mildew on Watermelon in Florida - UF/IFAS EDIS (PP342)

web.quote: There are three common causes of these patches in 2024: Take-all, Barley Yellow Dwarf Virus, and Fusarium head blight.
web.source_title: What Are the Pale Patches in Wheat Fields This Year? - Small Grains, NC State Extension

web.quote: Symptoms of barley yellow dwarf are highly variable and can be confused with those of wheat streak mosaic, nutrient deficiency, root and crown diseases, and environmental stress.
web.source_title: Barley Yellow Dwarf | CropWatch | University of Nebraska-Lincoln

wheat__barley-yellow-dwarf-virus__fusari.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

wheat__barley-yellow-dwarf-virus__loose-.member_b: Loose Smut Of Wheat Or Barley

web.quote: Symptoms of barley yellow dwarf are highly variable and can be confused with those of wheat streak mosaic, nutrient deficiency, root and crown diseases, and environmental stress.
web.source_title: Barley Yellow Dwarf | CropWatch | University of Nebraska-Lincoln

web.quote: FHB symptoms can be confused with various root and crown diseases, which cause entire heads to bleach.
web.source_title: Identification and Control of Fusarium Head Blight (Scab) of Wheat in Georgia | UGA CAES Field Report

wheat__fusarium-graminearum-schwabe__fus.member_b: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

wheat__fusarium-graminearum-schwabe__loo.member_b: Loose Smut Of Wheat Or Barley

web.quote: FHB symptoms can be confused with various root and crown diseases, which cause entire heads to bleach. Premature blight symptoms can also be confused with black chaff and glume blotch, which cause discoloration of the glumes and seed.
web.source_title: Identification and Control of Fusarium Head Blight (Scab) of Wheat in Georgia | CAES Field Report

web.quote: Members of the FHB complex can possibly survive as latent pathogens or silent endophytes in the leaves and potentially cause blotch-like symptoms that can be confounded with the typical blotch pathogens Z. tritici, P. tritici-repentis, and P. nodorum.
web.source_title: Diagnostic of head blight and leaf blotch diseases of wheat in Sweden using a mycobiome isolation approach (also published in European Journal of Plant Pathology, 2025)

wheat__fusarium-wilts--blights--rots-and.member_a: Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off
Fusarium Wilts, Blights, Rots And Damping-Off

web.quote: FHB causes bleaching of the wheat head whereas SNB produces purple to brown lesions on the glumes.
web.source_title: An Overview of Stagonospora Nodorum Leaf and Glume Blotch — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: One of the biggest problems identifying tan spot is its similarity to Stagonospora leaf blotch. However, it is difficult to tell the two diseases apart before pycnidia are produced in Stagonospora leaf blotch lesions or under dry conditions which may prevent pycnidia production.
web.source_title: Leaf Blotch Diseases of Wheat—Septoria tritici Blotch, Stagonospora nodorum Blotch and Tan Spot | Ohioline (Ohio State University Extension)

web.quote: Differentiating STB from SNB can be difficult and often requires microscopic observations.
web.source_title: Fungal Leaf Spot Diseases of Wheat: Tan spot, Septoria/Stagonospora nodorum blotch and Septoria tritici blotch | NDSU Agriculture

web.quote: Powdery mildew pustules can be big or small, developing into white-grayish cotton-like patches on leaves and stems. Small black dots (sexual structures) are often formed on the pustules. Pustules do not form stripes.
web.source_title: Stripe Rust Look-a-Likes | Stripe Rust | Washington State University
Wheat Diseases: Updated Facts and Pictures Part 1 | Agronomic Crops Network (Ohio State University Extension)

web.quote: Septoria tritici blotch ... symptoms commonly detected on lower leaves ... Powdery mildew develops as white, powdery lesions ... beginning on the surface of lower leaves and stems.
web.source_title: Leaf Blotch Diseases of Wheat—Septoria tritici Blotch, Stagonospora nodorum Blotch and Tan Spot (Ohioline, OSU)
web.claim: Both colonize the lower wheat canopy in cool, wet conditions; separated by superficial white powder vs embedded black pycnidia.

web.quote: The pustules occur in a random pattern and can be confused with other rust diseases, such as leaf rust.
web.source_title: An Overview of Stripe Rust of Wheat — Crop Protection Network

web.quote: Wheat streak mosaic disease can look similar to stripe rust, as both diseases manifest as yellow streaks with orange spores eventually appearing on the leaf when stripe rust occurs. ... If you go back the next day or two and you don't see orange stripes, it's probably not stripe rust.
web.source_title: Time to start scouting for wheat diseases - Farm Progress

web.quote: Tan spot and Septoria leaf spot are the two most common wheat leaf diseases in Montana. ... Leaf diseases can be confusing to diagnose because the symptoms of different diseases look very similar.
web.source_title: Fungal, Bacterial, and Physiological Leaf Diseases of Cereal Crops (wheat, durum, barley) - Montana State University Extension (MT200913AG)

web.quote: Differentiating the rust diseases can be difficult, but with practice they can be reliably identified. ... If the lesions are scattered on the affected plant parts, both stem rust and leaf rust are a possibility and additional characteristics must be considered.
web.source_title: Identifying Rust Diseases of Wheat and Barley (MF2919) — Kansas State University Research and Extension
